turns.content: What brings you in today?

Well, doctor, I've been having problems with my stomach for about a few weeks now. I feel sick almost every day, and some days I just can't keep any food down. I've also lost some weight, maybe about three kilos, and I didn't try to lose it.
When did this start?
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
It's mostly in my upper belly, right here under my ribs. It's not a sharp pain, more like a dull ache that stays most of the time. It gets worse when I feel really nauseous.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had a fever that I noticed. But sometimes I do feel a little hot or cold, especially after I throw up. But I didn't check my temperature or anything.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm taking metformin for my blood sugar, but that's all. I've been on it for a long time now, maybe a few years. I don't take anything else, not even vitamins. But I'm worried because my stomach feels worse after I eat, and sometimes even after taking the medicine.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

Yes, I have anemia. I've had it for a while, actually. The doctor told me my iron was low, so I take iron supplements sometimes, but not always. I thought maybe it was getting better, but now this stomach problem has me worried again.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I don't think so. I've never had a problem with any medicine before. I'm not sure about the iron pills, but I never had a reaction, so I guess not.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I tried not eating very much, just small bites of bland food like crackers or toast. And sometimes I drink ginger tea to settle my stomach a little. But nothing really makes it go away completely, it just sort of helps for a while.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Well, eating usually makes it worse. Especially if I eat anything heavy or greasy. The nausea gets much stronger then, and I might even throw up. Resting or not eating for a while helps a little, but the feeling never really goes away. It's worse in the morning, too.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been really hard, to be honest. I work as a bank teller, so I need to stand a lot and talk to people. But I feel so tired and sick, sometimes I just want to sit down. I've had to call in sick a few times, which I never used to do. I'm worried it's affecting my work and my energy is just not there like before.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

I used to have a glass of wine sometimes, but not anymore because my stomach feels so bad. I never was a heavy drinker. As for smoking, I only smoke a little, maybe a few cigarettes a day, but I've cut down because my appetite is so poor now.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

No, not that I know of. My mother had some stomach problems when she was older, but she never had this kind of nausea or vomiting like I do now. So I don't think it runs in the family or anything like that.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, I've never had anything like this before. I mean, I've had an upset stomach once in a while, but nothing that lasted for weeks and made me lose weight. This is really new for me, and it's scaring me a little.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, doctor, I think I understand. You're saying my stomach problems might be linked to the medicine or maybe something else, and you need to check more to find out the reason. I'm a little worried, but I'm glad you explained it clearly.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Do you have any questions for me?

Yes, I do have one question. Do you think this could be something serious, like an ulcer or... maybe something worse? I'm a little scared because I've never had this happen before, and I don't want to ignore it if it's something bad.
What do you think might be going on?

Well, I'm not a doctor, so I can't say for sure. But I'm worried it might be an ulcer or something with my stomach lining. Maybe it's the medicine I'm taking. Or... I don't know, maybe it's just a bad virus that won't go away. But I really hope it's nothing too serious. What do you think, doctor?
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, I think I've told you everything. I just really want to know what's wrong and get better. Thank you for listening, doctor. I feel a little better just talking about it.
